diagrams.title: CourseHub Data Model
diagrams.description: Entity Relationship Diagram showing the core database schema for users, courses, modules, and enrollments.
diagrams.mermaid_code:
erDiagram
    ENT-USER ||--o{ ENT-COURSE : "instructs"
    ENT-USER ||--o{ ENT-ENROLLMENT : "enrolls"
    ENT-COURSE ||--o{ ENT-MODULE : "contains"
    ENT-COURSE ||--o{ ENT-ENROLLMENT : "has"

    ENT-USER {
        int id PK
        string email
        string hashed_password
        string display_name
        string role
    }
    ENT-COURSE {
        int id PK
        int instructor_id FK
        string title
        string description
        int price_cents
        boolean published
    }
    ENT-MODULE {
        int id PK
        int course_id FK
        string title
        int order
    }
    ENT-ENROLLMENT {
        int id PK
        int student_id FK
        int course_id FK
        int progress
        datetime completed_at
    }

diagrams.title: User Registration & Welcome Flow
diagrams.description: Sequence diagram illustrating the asynchronous registration process and background email integration.
diagrams.mermaid_code:
sequenceDiagram
    actor Student
    participant API as "EP-AUTH-REG: /auth/register"
    participant DB as "PostgreSQL"
    participant BG as "FastAPI BackgroundTask"
    participant Email as "Resend Service"

    Student ->> API: POST /api/v1/auth/register
    API ->> DB: Create User (role=student)
    DB -->> API: User Created
    API ->> BG: add_task(send_welcome_email_async)
    API -->> Student: 201 Created (Access + Refresh Tokens)
    
    Note over BG, Email: Process runs asynchronously after response
    BG ->> Email: send_welcome_email_async(email, name)
    Email -->> BG: Email Sent

diagrams.title: Course Deletion Workflow
diagrams.description: Workflow for deleting a course, implementing the ActiveEnrollmentConstraint business rule.
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart TD
    START[Start Delete Request] --> AUTH{"Is User Instructor?"}
    
    AUTH -- "No" --> ERR403["Return 403 Forbidden"]
    AUTH -- "Yes" --> OWN{"Does User own Course?"}
    
    OWN -- "No" --> ERR403
    OWN -- "Yes" --> CHECK_ENROLL{"Active Enrollments Exist?"}
    
    CHECK_ENROLL -- "Yes" --> RULE_409["RULE-ACTIVE-ENROLL: Return 409 Conflict"]
    CHECK_ENROLL -- "No" --> DEL_PROC["Delete Course & Modules"]
    
    RULE_409 --> END[End]
    DEL_PROC --> SUCCESS["Return 204 No Content"]
    SUCCESS --> END
    ERR403 --> END

diagrams.title: Implementation Phase Traceability
diagrams.description: Traceability map of the project rollout phases and their dependencies.
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart LR
    subgraph Foundation
        PHASE-1["PHASE-1: Project Foundation & DB Schema"]
    end

    subgraph Security
        PHASE-2["PHASE-2: Auth Router & JWT Flow"]
    end

    subgraph Services
        PHASE-3["PHASE-3: Resend Email Integration"]
    end

    subgraph Domain
        PHASE-4["PHASE-4: Courses Router CRUD"]
        PHASE-5["PHASE-5: Enrolments Router"]
    end

    PHASE-1 --> PHASE-2
    PHASE-2 --> PHASE-3
    PHASE-2 --> PHASE-4
    PHASE-4 --> PHASE-5
